title: populus neapolitana
intro: Populus neapolitana, also known as Neapolitan poplar, is a deciduous tree native to southern Europe and southwestern Asia, characterized by its slender profile and heart-shaped leaves. The medicinal properties of Populus neapolitana lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The health benefits of this tree include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as aiding in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and various skin conditions. Populus neapolitana can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
